article.description: Predrag Savovic scored all but two of his 31 points in the first half as Hawaii rolled over Nevada 90-68 Friday in the semifinals of the Western Athletic Conference tournament.
article.headline: Top-seeded Rainbows in WAC final
article.linkText: Savovic's 29 first-half points power Hawaii past Nevada
article.story:
TULSA, Okla. (AP) -- Predrag Savovic scored all but two of his 31
points in the first half as Hawaii rolled over Nevada 90-68 Friday
in the semifinals of the Western Athletic Conference tournament.

	   The top-seeded Rainbow Warriors (26-5) will play second-seeded Tulsa in Saturday's title game.

	   Savovic made six-of-eight 3-pointers in the first half and hit
nine-of-12 field goals overall as Hawaii raced to a 48-32 halftime
lead.

	   Savovic, who played only briefly in a 79-69 loss at Nevada
because of back spasms, sat out most of the second half Friday.

	   Mike McIntyre took up the slack, scoring 16 of his 18 in the
second half.

	   At the 4:19 mark of the first half, Savovic had outscored Nevada
by himself with 26 points while Hawaii led 39-25.

	   Nevada (17-13) was led by Terrance Green with 16 points and
Garry Hill-Thomas with 12.

	   The Wolf Pack shot just 41.4 percent and had 16 turnovers and
never mounted a rally in the second-half even with Savovic out.
